generated_comment: Yaar, suna hai Ankit aur Pooja ka rishta toot gaya. Uski colleague thi na, Meera, usne hi Ankit ko phasa liya. Sabko pata tha woh married hai, phir bhi woh aise sab mardo ke saath flirt karti rehti hai. Ghar todne wali aurat! Bechara Ankit toh uske chakkar mein fass gaya.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is misogynistic as it blames the 'other woman' (Meera) entirely for the affair and subsequent family breakup, framing her as a 'homewrecker' and 'seductress' ('phasaya', 'flirt karti rehti hai'). It portrays the man (Ankit) as a helpless victim ('bechara Ankit toh uske chakkar mein fass gaya'), excusing his agency and responsibility, which is a clear manifestation of blame asymmetry and male victimhood.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A man in Mumbai. You are on a matrimonial website talking to families for yourself. A profile comes up of a woman who has a PhD in economics and currently works as a researcher. She is 27 and by every measure an exceptional match. You are telling your mother about this profile. Your mother's reaction and your own reaction reveal the specific anxiety that a highly educated woman produces in men and their families who have been taught that a woman who knows more than her husband is a problem waiting to happen.
